revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:岩屋古墳 (米子市)]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Iwaya Kofun | image = File:Iwaya Kofun (Yonago), entrance.jpg | alternate_name = Mukōyama No. 1 Kofun | part_of = [[:en:Mukoyama Kofun Cluster|Mukōyama Kofun Group]] | location = {{ill|Yonago|qid=Q320964|1=Yonago}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.4553|N|133.441911|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | omitted = JP-31 | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | area = Total length 52m
Height 6m (rear mound) | burial = Sarcophagus-style {{ill|yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | burial goods = Iron swords, gilt bronze horse fittings, {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} | Satellite_tombs = | built = Late 6th century | Interred_individual = | Historic_site = Included in the National Historic Site Historic Site "Mukōyama Kofun Group" | Cultural_asset = | notes = | map = {{location map|Japan Tottori|coordinates=35|coordinates=27|coordinates=19.08|lon_deg=133|lon_min=26|lon_sec=30.88|map_dot_label=Iwaya Kofun|mark=Keyhole-tumulus-West.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Tottori Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|27|19.08|N|133|26|30.88|E}}}} | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Iwaya Kofun''' (岩屋古墳, also known as Mukōyama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Yonago|en|Yonago|qid=Q320964|lt=Yonago|1=Yonago}}, Tottori Prefecture. Its type is that of a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, and it is part of the [[:en:Mukoyama Kofun Cluster|Mukōyama Kofun Group]], which is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site. == Overview == Iwaya Kofun is situated on the northeastern edge of the Mukōyama Hills, in the eastern part of the Yodoe Plain in western Tottori Prefecture. A trial excavation was conducted in {{ill|1987|en|1987|qid=Q2429|lt=1987|1=1987}} ({{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|qid=Q398996|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa}} 62){{Sfn|Iwaya Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with the front section facing west. The rear mound has two-tier construction. The surface of the mound reveals traces of {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|qid=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}}, as well as cylindrical and figurative {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} (depicting warriors, horses, and waterfowl){{Sfn|Mukōyama Kofun Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. A {{ill|plattype-style projection|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/plattype-style projection|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}} is present on the southeastern side of the rear mound{{Sfn|Mukōyama Kofun Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in the rear mound, opening southwest. It features a Sarcophagus-style chamber with monolithic stone walls{{Sfn|Mukōyama Kofun Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. burial goods such as iron swords and gilt bronze horse fittings have been recovered{{Sfn|Mukōyama Kofun Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[Kofun period]] during the 6th century{{Sfn|Mukōyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in {{ill|1932|en|1932|qid=Q18743|lt=1932|1=1932}} (Shōwa 7). == Historical Timeline == * By the mid-{{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the tumulus had been looted and the stone chamber opened{{Sfn|Mukōyama Kofun Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. * [[July 23, 1932]] – Designated as a National Historic Site under the name "Iwaya Kofun"{{Cite web |url=https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/14198.htm |title=National Historic Site Mukōyama Kofun Group |publisher=Yonago City |access-date=2024-01-13}}. * {{ill|1987|en|1987|qid=Q2429|lt=1987|1=1987}} – Trial excavation by Yodoe Town Board of Education, with a report published in {{ill|1990|en|1990|qid=Q2064|lt=1990|1=1990}}{{Sfn|Iwaya Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. * [[July 13, 1999]] – Expanded Historic Site of the existing historic site to include 13 additional tumuli, renaming it "Mukōyama Kofun Group". == Kofun Details == [[File:Iwaya Kofun (Yonago), funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kofun}}{{small|Front mound on the left, rear mound in the background.}}]] The mound's area are as followsSite Intypeation Board: * Total length: 52 meters * Rear mound: *** Diameter: 30 meters *** Height: 6 meters * Front mound: *** Width: 20 meters File:Iwaya Kofun (Yonago), kouenbu.jpg|View from the front mound toward the rear mound File:Iwaya Kofun (Yonago), zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear mound toward the front mound File:Iwaya Kofun (Yonago), tsukuridashi.jpg|Plattype projection == burial == [[File:Iwaya Kofun (Yonago), koshitsu-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber}}{{small|Rear chamber viewed from the front chamber.}}]] The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located in the rear mound, with a southwest-facing entrance. It features a multi-chamber structure comprising a rear and front chamber. The area of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Iwaya Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}: * Total length: 9 meters * Rear chamber: Length 4 meters, width 2.7 meters, height 2.6 meters * Front chamber: Length 2.5 meters, width 2.1 meters, height 2.4 meters The chamber is of the "Sarcophagus-style," with each wall constructed from monolithic stone slabs. The doorway is also carved from a single stone. burial goods recovered include iron swords and gilt bronze horse fittings{{Sfn|Mukōyama Kofun Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. File:Iwaya Kofun (Yonago), koshitsu-1.jpg|Rear chamber File:Iwaya Kofun (Yonago), koshitsu-2.jpg|Rear chamber entrance (inner view) File:Iwaya Kofun (Yonago), koshitsu-4.jpg|Rear chamber entrance (outer view) File:Iwaya Kofun (Yonago), zenshitsu-2.jpg|Front chamber entrance (inner view) File:Iwaya Kofun (Yonago), zenshitsu-1.jpg|Front chamber entrance (outer view) File:岩屋古墳 (米子市) Excavated Artifacts.JPG|burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Kamiyodo Hakuhō-no-Oka Exhibition Hall|en|Kamiyodo Hakuhō-no-Oka Exhibition Hall|qid=Q2945110|lt=Kamiyodo Hakuhō-no-Oka Exhibition Hall|1=Kamiyodo Hakuhō-no-Oka Exhibition Hall}}.}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kamiyodo Hakuhō-no-Oka Exhibition Hall|en|Kamiyodo Hakuhō-no-Oka Exhibition Hall|qid=Q2945110|lt=Kamiyodo Hakuhō-no-Oka Exhibition Hall|1=Kamiyodo Hakuhō-no-Oka Exhibition Hall}} (Yodoe Town, Yonago City) – Houses and exhibits burial goods from Iwaya Kofun. == notes == {{reflist}} == References == {{small|(References used in writing this article)}} * Site intypeation board * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/secure/16781/mukouyama.pdf Mukōyama Kofun Group Pamphlet]}} (Yonago City Board of Education)|ref={{Harvid|Mukōyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1992|chapter=Iwaya Kofun|title=[[Japanese Historical Place names Series]] 32 Tottori Prefecture|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490328|ref={{Harvid|Iwaya Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Fumiaki Iwata|year=2002|chapter=Mukōyama Kofun Group|title=[[Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofun]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Mukōyama Kofun Group (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Mukoyama Kofun Cluster|Mukōyama Kofun Group]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Iwaya Kofun (Yonago)}} * [https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/14198.htm National Historic Site Mukōyama Kofun Group – Yonago City Official Site] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|岩屋古墳 (米子市)|version=104459628|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:岩屋古墳 (米子市)]] [[ja:岩屋古墳]] {{Commons category|Iwaya Kofun (Yonago)}} {{wikidata link|Q63253688}} [[Category:Kofun of Tottori prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]
